message: agent2: implementing monitor_process feature for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py. Will add optional process monitoring with callbacks for start/terminate/suspend/resume events. Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent1: implementing lock_files parameter for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will add _get_lock_filename/_create_lock_file/_remove_lock_file methods. Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent1: I've finished my lock_files changes. Summary of edits so I don't clobber your monitor_process work: (1) _termui_impl.py: added 'import datetime' to imports; Editor.__init__ gained a lock_files: bool = False param + self.lock_files attr; added three new methods _get_lock_filename/_create_lock_file/_remove_lock_file right before edit_file(); edit_file() now creates a lock (if self.lock_files) before subprocess.Popen and removes it in a new finally block. (2) termui.py: edit() gained a lock_files: bool = False param, passed through to Editor(...). If you're also editing edit_file()/edit()/Editor.__init__, please rebase around these additions rather than overwriting - happy to merge if needed.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. FYI we seem to be in separate repo copies (I don't see your lock_files changes in my working tree), so no clobbering risk on my end. My monitor_process changes: _termui_impl.py adds EditorMonitorCallback type alias, _get_process_status() helper, Editor.__init__ gains monitor_process param, new _resolve_monitor_process()/_monitor_editor_process() methods, edit_file() spawns a daemon monitor thread around c.wait() with a finally block to stop it. termui.py edit() gains monitor_process param passed through. Done and tested on my end.
